sanskrit: वेदाविनाशिनं नित्यं य एनमजमव्ययम् । कथं स पुरुषः पार्थ कं घातयति हन्ति कम् ॥
hindiTranslation: हे पृथानन्दन जो मनुष्य इस शरीरीको अविनाशी, नित्य, जन्मरहित और अव्यय जानता है, वह कैसे किसको मारे और कैसे किसको मरवाये ॥
englishTranslation: One who knows the imperishable, eternal, unborn, and unchanging cannot think of killing or causing death.
essence: Clear seeing makes violence look absurd.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Anyone who truly sees the inner being as unborn and unchanging cannot think in terms of killing or being killed.
simpleInsight: The fear around death loosens when you see what cannot be destroyed.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen before battle. Krishna deepens the teaching: the true inner being is not touched by birth or death. From that vision, the idea of killing becomes confused, because the real person was never perishable.
modernRelevance: You are about to send the message that ends a relationship, or stay silent and let harm continue. This verse asks you to see deeper than the body of the moment and act from clear seeing, not panic.
translationLiteral: वेद (knows) / अविनाशिनम् (the imperishable) / नित्यम् (the eternal) / यः (who) / एनम् (this one) / अजम् (unborn) / अव्ययम् (unchanging) / कथम् (how) / सः (that) / पुरुषः (person) / पार्थ (Arjuna) / कम् (whom) / घातयति (causes to be killed) / हन्ति (kills) / कम् (whom)
